text: This study aimed to analyze the implementation of Religious Program and Deepening of Scriptural Books (AKPK) policy in SMPN 1 Darangdan, an educational institution which located in Purwakarta Regency that is designed to meet the needs of the students’ religious literacy and to avoid religious intolerance. This study uses a qualitative descriptive approach by collecting the data through observations, interviews, and documents analysis to determine the management and the implications of the implementation of AKPK towards the students’ values. The results of this study indicated that the implementation of AKPK policy in SMPN 1 Darangdan is organized using a form of planning, organizing, actuating, and controlling (POAC). This policy consists of the preparedness planning prior to the beginning of the regular classes, the task assignments given to several parts of schools organization to carry out the worship activities such as Duha prayer, and other supporting tools utilized by students, teachers, and schools managers. The successful implementation of AKPK policy is also reinforced by several instruments, including the use of the digital and print versions of the guidebooks, the management accountability given to the manager, and the active participation of all teachers in implementing the AKPK policy. This study further found the obstacles faced by schools in implementing this policy, namely time allocation and the Arabic fluency difficulties faced by students. The school responded to this challenge by adding an additional learning hour as special lesson aimed at dealing with the Arabic literacy difficulties faced by students. The implementation of AKPK policy activities is shown to develop the students’ spiritual and social values so that they become better individuals in their interactions with the Almighty as well as with the society around them and at the same time helps to curb potential social unrest arising from religious intolerance.
